what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a comment/point) by Tim in the dragnet-development channel, replying to item-5c2d936d and extending a forgiving-release interaction rule to tapping.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-ebf0c8e8, board:// address, type note, source claude_code, state posted, title 'Tim · point', author_session tim, channel dragnet-development.
It links to board://item-5c2d936d with kind commented_on.
Created and updated timestamps are identical: 2026-06-24T07:59:29.353666+00:00.
History shows a single 'filed' transition from null to posted by tim.
Body uses a [point] marker and agrees that release forgiveness for thumb drift should also apply to tapping, so precision should not be required.
standouts: Extends the prior 'forgiving release' guideline to tap interactions.
Authored as a point/comment reply to another noticeboard item.
Emphasizes committing to whatever the current UI state/chip shows rather than requiring precise lift/tap placement.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and markdown body.
Uses board:// URI addressing and a commented_on link kind.
Uses a [point] marker for the note type/tone.
notes: A brief design note arguing that tap and release interactions should tolerate imprecise finger placement and commit to the currently shown level/state.
questions: What specific UI control or gesture is being designed (e.g., slider, picker, button)?
What does 'the chip currently says' refer to in the underlying system?
Which item-5c2d936d requirement is being replied to, and what is its exact text?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (a posted note/point reply) matches the content: it records Tim's agreement and extension of the forgiving-release principle to taps.
intention_signals: It appears to be capturing a design guideline that both release and tap interactions should be forgiving of finger drift and should commit to the currently displayed value/state rather than requiring a precise lift or tap location.
novelty: It generalizes a specific release-forgiveness rule into a broader tap-forgiveness principle.
contribution: It contributes a UX guideline that tap/release interactions should tolerate positional imprecision and commit to the current on-screen state, which a fused system would keep as an interaction-design constraint.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard point by Tim replying to item-5c2d936d, stating that tap interactions should be as forgiving as releases and should not require precise finger placement.
intention_for_embedding: To record and propagate a design principle that both tap and release gestures should commit to the current UI state regardless of small finger drift.
